mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in real activity-sensitive retail sales indicates near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, so a positive 3-month trend in RSAFS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:RSAFS latest available value was 456321 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the sign of 3-month RSAFS growth has a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 for 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in FEDFUNDS from the zero-rate area increases the discount rate applied to equities before earnings expectations adjust, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after a policy-rate crossing.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its 12-month moving average by more than 0.10pp
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 0.4 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is already low and payroll employment keeps expanding, labor-market tightness raises expected wage costs and margin pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is below 5.0 and PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
series:PAYEMS
fact:UNRATE latest available value was 4.9 as of t-1mo
fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 144598 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if low-UNRATE positive-PAYEMS episodes have average 60-day S&P 500 returns equal to or above the unconditional average in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: An upside turn in industrial production signals improving operating leverage and stronger goods-sector earnings expectations, so a positive INDPRO growth crossing predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth crosses from negative to positive
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:INDPRO latest available value was 104.4016 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO growth crossings have a 20-day hit rate below 50% and a mean return below the unconditional 20-day mean.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction activity, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 6-month moving average by more than 10%
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:HOUST latest available value was 1211 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST declines are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or by returns above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%, real short rates fall and liquidity remains supportive, so this interaction predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above 2.0% while FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 239.828 as of t-2mo
fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 0.4 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL acceleration and low-FEDFUNDS interaction has a nonpositive coefficient for 20-day returns in walk-forward regressions over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment from a low level signals deterioration in household income and recession risk, causing investors to mark down earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:UNRATE latest available value was 4.9 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE 3-month increases above 0.3pp are not followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth confirms that aggregate demand is expanding and reduces near-term recession probability, so an improving GDPC1 growth trend predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
fact:GDPC1 latest available value was 16570.2 as of t-5mo
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth trend has a 5-day walk-forward Rank IC below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window or if positive-growth quarters do not outperform negative-growth quarters.
